title: Cynanchica capitellata

taxonomy: Cynanchica capitellata is classified within the Apocynaceae family, which is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This plant is a member of the genus Cynanchica, which is typically associated with twining vines and shrubs. The species C. capitellata is native to the Americas and is often found in dry, sandy areas. The classification of C. capitellata is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Magnoliophyta, which includes flowering plants. The plant's unique characteristics and growth habits are reflective of its classification within the Apocynaceae family.

Further studies are needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Cynanchica capitellata for medicinal use.
